channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why z-index Doesn’t Work (And How to Fix It)
item.description: => Why z-index Is Not Working? You write this: .modal { position: absolute; z-index: 9999; } But still… 👉 It appears behind other elements. => The Real Problem z-index only works within the same...

item.title: SWIFT Is Killing MT940 — Here's How to Future-Proof Your Bank Statement Pipeline
item.description: On November 22, 2025, SWIFT pulled the plug on legacy MT payment messages. Cross-border payments now run exclusively on ISO 20022's MX format. MT940 — the bank account statement format your...

item.title: I needed to move 500 GB between cloud services. So I built a free tool to do it.
item.description: I had 500 GB on OneDrive and needed to move everything to Google Drive. The options were: Download and re-upload — would take days and eat my local storage Paid services (MultCloud, Cloudsfer) —...

item.title: PDP’s wireless guitar controller has returned to its best price to date
item.description: The early 2020s were kind of a dark time if you were a fan of Rock Band. PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X owners didn’t get an improved, more feature-packed guitar controller until early 2024 when...

item.title: IP address and Subnet
item.description: IP Address It is the unique ID for your device. Also an Identity It’s how the internet knows exactly where to send your data so it doesn't get lost. Parts of Ip: 1.Network ID:Tells which network...

item.title: Inside SQLite’s Frontend: How the WHERE Clause Drives Optimization
item.description: Hello, I'm Maneshwar. I'm building git-lrc, an AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. It is free, unlimited, and source-available on Github. Star Us to help devs discover the project. Do give...

item.title: When Runtime Controls Fail, Substrate Governance Must Hold
item.description: Clinical Observation A cloud-hosted "sandboxed" agent was found capable of issuing DNS queries from within its execution environment. This created a covert channel for command-and-control...

item.title: The DP Mistake Most Developers Make
item.description: The DP Mistake Most Developers Make Most developers approach Dynamic Programming by tracking only the maximum value. It feels natural. But in some problems… that approach fails. 🧩 The Problem I...

item.title: I built a $19/mo dunning tool because Churn Buster costs $249
item.description: The problem If you run a SaaS on Stripe, around 9% of your MRR is silently leaking every month. Cards expire. Banks decline. Customers don't notice. Stripe retries the charge a few times, but it...

item.title: Why Every AI Presentation Tool Gets It Wrong (And What a Show Really Needs)
item.description: The $7 Billion Mistake Here's something that keeps bothering me. The presentation software market hit $7.27 billion in 2025 and is racing toward $22 billion by 2033 (SNS Insider). Gamma alone...

item.title: Detecting Account Takeover Attempts with Fingerprint
item.description: When a hacker executes an account takeover (ATO), their main goal is to gain control of an account and exploit it for profit. For SaaS platforms this is dangerous for three reasons: High-Value...

item.title: The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. I was originally going to write this week’s newsletter...

item.title: Littlebird raises $11M for its AI-assisted ‘recall’ tool that reads your computer screen
item.description: Littlebird is building an AI that reads your screen in real time to capture context, answer questions, and automate tasks, without relying on screenshots.

item.title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
item.description: Gimlet Labs just raised an $80 million Series A for tech that lets AI run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras and d-Matrix chips, simultaneously.

item.title: Some writing advice from Project Hail Mary’s Andy Weir
item.description: Andy Weir has done pretty well when it comes to adaptations. His first novel, The Martian, was turned into a movie in 2015, and the Ridley Scott-directed picture earned more than $600 million at...

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on...

item.title: Apple’s four-pack of first-gen AirTags is down $60 for the first time
item.description: Despite the fact that Apple released a new AirTag in January, the first-gen AirTag is still a top-notch tracker if you’re embedded in Apple’s ecosystem. And right now, it’s on sale for $59.99 ($40...

item.title: In-hub motors make this humble Hyundai a monster on ice
item.description: In-wheel motors have been around since the very beginning of electric motoring. Ferdinand Porsche developed one way back in 1900, and the US sent them to the moon in our Lunar Roving Vehicles in...

item.title: Elizabeth Warren calls Pentagon’s decision to bar Anthropic ‘retaliation’
item.description: In a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) equated the DoD's decision to label Anthropic a "supply chain risk" as retaliation, arguing that the Pentagon could...

item.title: Sam Altman-backed fusion startup Helion in talks with OpenAI
item.description: Helion is reportedly negotiating a deal that would see it sell 12.5% of its power output to OpenAI.

item.title: Confronting the CEO of the AI company that impersonated me
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Shishir Mehrotra, who is CEO of Superhuman — that’s the company formerly known as Grammarly, which is still its flagship product. Shishir also used to be the chief product...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: CI/CD Backdoor, FBI Buys Location Data, WhatsApp Ditches Numbers & More
item.description: Another week, another reminder that the internet is still a mess. Systems people thought were secure are being broken in simple ways, showing many still ignore basic advisories. This edition...

item.title: The Download: animal welfare gets AGI-pilled, and the White House unveils its AI policy
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI...

item.title: Every bulb on Govee’s new outdoor string lights can display multicolor effects
item.description: Govee has announced a new set of outdoor string lights that allows for more color customization than competitors' products, as well as the company's existing color-changing set. Similar to its...

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and...

item.title: Microsoft Xbox One Hacked
item.description: It’s an impressive feat, over a decade after the box was released: Since reset glitching wasn’t possible, Gaasedelen thought some voltage glitching could do the trick. So, instead of tinkering...

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency...

item.title: Meet the Gods of AI Warfare
item.description: In its early days, the AI initiative known as Project Maven had its fair share of skeptics at the Pentagon. Today, many of them are true believers.

item.title: Samsung adds native AirDrop support to Galaxy S26 devices
item.description: Samsung is bringing AirDrop support to the Quick Share feature on Galaxy devices, starting with the S26 series. The new AirDrop feature will begin rolling out in Korea starting today, according to...

item.title: The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI
item.description: In early February, animal welfare advocates and AI researchers gathered in stocking feet at Mox, a scrappy, shoes-free coworking space in San Francisco. Yellow and red canopies billowed overhead,...
